notes: cause: Clang could not find a declaration for a type name. For kernel-side types (`struct task_struct`, `struct sock`, BPF map definitions), this usually means vmlinux.h is missing or stale. For Horizon-emitted types (event structs, context structs), it may indicate the emitter did not declare the type in scope at this point.
suggest: If `task_struct` is a kernel type, regenerate vmlinux.h against your target kernel (`bpftool btf dump file /sys/kernel/btf/vmlinux format c > vmlinux.h`) and include it. If it is a Horizon-emitted type, verify the corresponding `type` or capability declaration appears before its first use in the .hzn source. If neither applies, capture the generated C and file a bug — the emitter may have dropped a declaration.
